What is a nurse union?
It’s a group of nurses who come together to speak as one voice to protect staffing ratios, patient safety, pay, and working conditions.

🧠 Why do babies go through withdrawal after birth? Here's the analogy that makes it click.
When opioids cross the placenta during pregnancy, baby's nervous system adapts — they get used to having them. Then the cord is cut, the supply stops, and the volume slams to max. 😳
That

🚨 Adrenal Crisis Nursing Priorities
✅ Rapidly recognize the signs
✅ Replace volume → get that BP up
✅ Correct sodium & glucose
✅ Give stress-dose IV hydrocortisone
Adrenal crisis happens when the body doesn’t have enough cortisol to respond to stress. Without prompt treatment, p

Being ready for the NCLEX® doesn’t always look loud.
It’s easy to see people online sharing how many hours they studied, how many questions they did, or how confident they feel. But your prep doesn’t have to look like anyone else’s to be real.

This isn't just anxiety. This is serotonin syndrome — and it can happen fast when meds get stacked.

🚗 Red blood cells are the Uber drivers of your body.
Their job? Deliver oxygen to every tissue and organ that needs it.
When anemia hits, those drivers start disappearing—and oxygen delivery slows down. The result? Fatigue, weakness, shortness of breath, and tissues that aren’t
Don’t self doubt while taking the NCLEX - you got this! #archernursing #nclex #nursetok

📚 Placenta Previa vs. Abruption
🔹 Previa = Location problem
Placenta covers the cervix → bright red, painless bleeding
🔹 Abruption = Attachment problem
Placenta detaches → dark red bleeding, tender uterus, fetal distress
Remember:
Previa = Wrong location
Abruption = Wrong sepa

Blood Products Explained 🩸

🩸Whole Blood → Contains all components, RBCs, WBCs, platelets, and plasma (it’s whole!) 

🩸Packed Red Blood Cells (PRBCs) → Red blood cells only (separated from platelets and plasma)

🩸Platelets (PLTs) → Platelets ONLY 

🩸Fresh Frozen Plasma (FFP)

SAS Method 🧼
(Scrub, Administer, Scrub)

Why you can't stop blood pressure meds cold turkey ❌🫀
 
When blood pressure medication is stopped abruptly instead of being tapered, the pressure doesn't simply return to baseline—it snaps right past it. Educate your patients to always taper slowly.
